denied_services.denied_services_line_1: Denial of exception request for continuation of adalimumab (Humira) 40 mg subcutaneous injection every 2 weeks for treatment of severe seropositive rheumatoid arthritis, originally approved from 2022-03-15 through 2024-09-30; denial issued on 2024-10-05 for coverage beyond 2024-09-30.
denied_services.denied_services_line_2: Health plan has required a step-therapy change to a different preferred biologic (etanercept) despite documented prior inadequate response and intolerable side effects to etanercept in 2021, including increased joint swelling and injection-site reactions requiring urgent care on 2021-11-02.
denied_services.denied_services_line_3: Request for coverage includes associated services: medication administration supplies, necessary laboratory monitoring (CBC, CMP, and inflammatory markers every 12 weeks), and rheumatology follow-up visits every 3–4 months at Austin Rheumatology Associates.
denied_services.denied_services_line_4: The denial affects ongoing maintenance therapy that has kept the patient’s disease stable and allowed him to continue working full time; interruption already resulted in increased morning stiffness, swollen joints in hands and wrists, and missed work days between 2024-10-20 and 2024-11-15 while relying on short prednisone tapers prescribed as a temporary measure.
